# Version Retrospective – v1.5.0
This document reflects on what worked, what didn't, and how future versions can be improved.

## Version Summary
v1.5.0 added a **Code View** popup that shows the exact Anthropic API call for the user's current configuration in TypeScript, Python, and curl, and introduced **Prism 1.30.0** (self-hosted) for syntax highlighting across the Code View snippets and the Full I/O viewer JSON. Also added per-block copy buttons, a global "Wrap code" toggle on the Trace pane, a session-scoped "Original prompt" toggle that substitutes the user's most recent chat prompt into the snippet, and a top-nav redesign (pill cluster with SVG icons, selected from a 4-variation mockup).

Shipped 2026-04-17. All 3 planned features (F33, F34, F35) delivered, plus enhancements discovered during the build that became part of the scope: top-nav pill-cluster redesign (mid-phase UI overhaul), global Trace-pane wrap toggle (replaced an earlier per-section variant), sentinel-stripping for the system prompt in generated snippets, arrow-key tab navigation, and the Original-prompt checkbox (added at end of Phase 6).

## What Went Well

- **Mockup-first on UI decisions paid off twice.** The 3-mockup Code View modal evaluation (centered modal / slide panel / full-screen sidebar) and the 4-mockup top-nav button evaluation (ghost / pill cluster / tinted / labeled) both resolved design disagreements before code got written. User picked Mockup A and V2 respectively; both were strong defaults, the alternatives were useful negative space.
- **Phase 3 validated the foundation before Phase 4-5 built on it.** Shipping Prism + copy buttons on the existing Full I/O viewer first (easier surface, smaller scope) meant the Code View modal inherited working CSS, a tested copy helper, and a known-good Prism theme. Phase 4 modal shell was mostly structural; Phase 5 focused on snippet generation without fighting rendering details.
- **Honest scope pushback on "Include API key" checkbox.** User asked for two checkboxes; I pushed back on inlining the API key and explained the secret-scanning / sharing risk. User agreed. Shipped "Original prompt" only. Good example of the user treating me as a sounding board — they brought the idea, I brought the objection, we landed in the right place without churn.
- **End-to-end curl validation.** Test 5.7 ran the generated curl against the real Anthropic API twice — once with a trivial prompt (no advisor call fired, matching the Recommended preset's timing guidance) and once with a substantive prompt (advisor fired, full `advisor_message` iteration in the response). That's the strongest possible validation for the curl path; it proved the shell escaping, JSON escaping, and heredoc construction all survived round-trip through the shell.
- **Pre-change diagnosis of the "wrap horizontal scroll" bug.** User reported the horizontal scrollbar only appearing at the bottom of the snippet. Correctly diagnosed as a nested-scroll-container problem (scrollbar on the pre, pre taller than its scrolling parent). Fix was two CSS lines: hoist horizontal scroll to the panel with `min-width: max-content` on the pre.
- **Lazy initialization sidestepped a real TDZ bug.** `makeCopyButton` calls into a `const COPY_ICON_SVG` declared later in the file. At module-load time the copy button creation hit the TDZ because I was eager-creating it. User reported "button does nothing". Moved creation into a lazy `ensureCodeViewCopyBtn()` called from `openCodeView`. Fix took 5 minutes once the error message was visible.

## What Could Have Gone Better

- **Prism theme selector drift.** The trace pane's wrap toggle initially had the `white-space: pre-wrap` rule only on `<pre>`, not on the inner `<code>`. Prism's theme sets `white-space: pre` on both. The `<code>` rule won and wrap didn't work. User caught it immediately ("wrap does not work"). Fix was one line. Lesson: when an inner element re-declares a property the parent inherits, overrides need to target both.
- **curl heredoc shell escaping took two iterations.** Initial attempt used unquoted `<<EOF` with a `__PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER__` swap. Worked for the simple cases but a system prompt containing `$` would have expanded in the shell. Added `escapeForBashHeredoc()` to escape `\`, `$`, `` ` ``. Got the edge cases right the second time. Should have anticipated special-character handling on the first design pass — I was focused on JSON correctness and missed that the JSON was going through a second parser (the shell) with its own interpretation of those same characters.
- **Three separate "wrap" toggles by the end of the version.** Initially added a per-section wrap toggle on the Full I/O viewer, then refactored to a global Trace-pane toggle (user asked for this), then added a separate Code-View toggle (scoped correctly from the start this time). The per-section one was dead code for about 30 minutes before I deleted it. Should have asked "should this be global?" before implementing the per-section version.
- **TypeScript and Python snippets were structurally validated but not executed.** Only curl was end-to-end tested against the real API. Honest in the release notes about this — but future versions should either include Node/Python smoke-test invocations in the test plan or explicitly mark TS/Python as "visually validated". A 5-minute `npx tsx` or `python file.py` run would have bumped confidence significantly.
- **`node --check` doesn't catch TDZ errors.** I ran syntax validation on app.js after the phase 5 changes and it passed. But the TDZ issue only surfaces at module load in the browser. Static check was not enough. Should have loaded the page as part of the "I wrote new code" loop instead of waiting for the user to click the button and report the failure.

## Lessons Learned

- **Scroll hierarchy is a design decision, not a CSS afterthought.** Nested scroll containers (panel scrolls vertically, pre inside scrolls horizontally) feel correct at the CSS level but fail UX when the inner container is taller than the outer. Always hoist horizontal scroll to the same container as vertical when the content is stacked tall — single always-visible scrollbar.
- **Generated code is leaky code.** Any snippet we emit will be pasted somewhere the user hasn't thought about: pastebin, Stack Overflow, git, screenshots, screenshots of screenshots. Defaults matter disproportionately. `process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in every example is a convention because it's safe by default. Breaking that convention (even with an opt-in) is an educational step backward.
- **Playground-specific conventions need to be stripped at the export boundary.** The `<!-- advisor:only -->` sentinels exist for the app's compare-mode server logic. They're inert to Anthropic but noise to a user copying code. Caught this before ship by noticing the Recommended preset text was leaking into Code View snippets. Stripping at the `snapshotCodeViewSettings()` boundary was the right layer — keeps the raw textarea state intact for preset detection but cleans the output that goes into snippets.
- **Mockups are cheap; design arguments are expensive.** The 4-variation top-nav mockup took 10 minutes to write. It eliminated a back-and-forth that would otherwise have been "here's my idea" / "here's my counter" / "let me try yet another thing". Even when I thought V3 was best, seeing V1–V4 side-by-side was what made V2 obviously right to the user.
- **When a user reports a bug with a confusing phrasing, demand the console.** "The code button is not bringing up the code window" — I spent ~2 minutes speculating about event handlers, hoist order, CSS. When the user pasted the actual `Uncaught ReferenceError: Cannot access 'COPY_ICON_SVG' before initialization`, the fix became obvious instantly. Always ask for the error text first.

## Action Items

- **Validate TS and Python snippets end-to-end** in a future version. Either add them to the test plan as explicit runs, or build a small CI-style smoke test that imports the SDK, substitutes a fake API key, and catches SDK-signature drift at PR-time.
- **Consider persisting the Trace-pane "Wrap code" toggle** to `localStorage`. Currently session-only, matching `Sync panes`. User preference around wrap is likely stickier than sync-panes (which is tied to current workflow). Not urgent — wait for a user complaint.
- **Keep the Prism version pinned for at least 12 months** before re-evaluating. The `vendor/prism/README.md` documents the source URLs for regeneration; no auto-upgrade path. If Prism 2.x ships, re-run the theme tuning against the new token class names.
- **Potential future version candidates** (no items currently in the top-level backlog):
  - `streaming` — the advisor sub-inference doesn't stream, but the executor's final continuation does. Progressive output on the last iteration would improve perceived latency on long responses. Non-trivial — requires SSE wiring.
  - `prompt cache breakpoints on the executor` — explicitly out-of-scope in the PRD (v1.5.0 PRD §"Out of Scope"), but worth revisiting when Anthropic ships a concrete use case.
  - `multi-turn welcome slideshow` — the v1.4.0 retrospective flagged this as an action item; still not done. A 3-turn conversation demo would show the advisor firing multiple times, which current single-turn examples don't reliably do.
